question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: High: Temporary freezing of network transactions by delaying one block by 500% or more of the average block time of the preceding 24 hours beyond standard difficulty adjustments'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the merge function (_, b) -> b in the Collectors.toMap() call to overwrite the local cluster's real high-lag value with a low-lag duplicate entry injected later in the series list, causing the health check to return UP despite a 500%+ block time delay?
